messages.content:
Текст вопроса
Match the movie genres with their definitions
a film that copies something serious or important and makes it seem silly
Ответ 1 Вопрос 1
a film that tells a long story about brave actions and exciting events
Ответ 2 Вопрос 1
a film that deals with subjects such as evil, moral problems etc, often using a story about people involved in a crime and filmed in a way that seems dark or filled with shadows
Ответ 3 Вопрос 1
a film or television programme that is based on a book or play
Ответ 4 Вопрос 1
a film which involves humorous acting in which the performers fall over, throw things at each other etc

Ответ 1 Вопрос 1: a film that copies something serious or important and makes it seem silly - Parody
Ответ 2 Вопрос 1: a film that tells a long story about brave actions and exciting events - Epic
Ответ 3 Вопрос 1: a film that deals with subjects such as evil, moral problems etc, often using a story about people involved in a crime and filmed in a way that seems dark or filled with shadows - Film noir
Ответ 4 Вопрос 1: a film or television programme that is based on a book or play - Adaptation

Match the words with their definitions:

sound engineer
screenwriter
editor
stuntman
lighting technician
director

someone who operates consoles and other equipment to control, replay, and mix sound from various sources in live concert performances and in the production of records, tapes, and films
Ответ 1 Вопрос 2
a man who is employed to take the place of an actor when something dangerous has to be done in a film
Ответ 2 Вопрос 2
someone who prepares a film, television programme, or sound recording for broadcasting by deciding what to include and checking for any mistakes
Ответ 3 Вопрос 2
the person who gives instructions to the actors and other people working on a film or play
Ответ 4 Вопрос 2
someone who handles all of the electrical needs as well as places and focuses all of the lighting under the direction of the gaffer
Ответ 5 Вопрос 2
someone who writes plays for film or television 
Ответ 6 Вопрос 2

Ответ 1 Вопрос 2: sound engineer
Ответ 2 Вопрос 2: stuntman
Ответ 3 Вопрос 2: editor
Ответ 4 Вопрос 2: director
Ответ 5 Вопрос 2: lighting technician
Ответ 6 Вопрос 2: screenwriter

Put the phrases in the correct places:

down payment
down-to-earth
down the drain
ups and downs.
down-at-heel
down-and-out
down in the mouth
hand-me-downs

But he will still be organising 13,000 volunteers into bringing some kind of Christmas to 125,000 needy, lonely or  Blank 1 Вопрос 1 people.
The town today is a shabby,  Blank 2 Вопрос 1 place.
We’ve almost got enough money to make a  Blank 3 Вопрос 1 on a house.
Well that’s it. 18 months’ work  Blank 4 Вопрос 1 .
I always had to wear my sister's  Blank 5 Вопрос 1 .
Fran’s a friendly,  Blank 6 Вопрос 1 person.
We have our  Blank 7 Вопрос 1 like all couples.
Tim’s looking very  Blank 8 Вопрос 1 .

But he will still be organising 13,000 volunteers into bringing some kind of Christmas to 125,000 needy, lonely or down-and-out people.
The town today is a shabby, down-at-heel place.
We’ve almost got enough money to make a down payment on a house.
Well that’s it. 18 months’ work down the drain.
I always had to wear my sister's hand-me-downs.
Fran’s a friendly, down-to-earth person.
We have our ups and downs like all couples.
Tim’s looking very down in the mouth.

Fill in the gap with an appropriate preposition:

As their smaller competitors go Ответ Вопрос 2 , the large home-building companies grab more and more of the marke
As their smaller competitors go out of business, the large home-building companies grab more and more of the market.

Fill in the gap with an appropriate preposition: go up go under go over go on pick up pick at pick on pick out

As their smaller competitors go …the large home-building companies grab more and more of the marke
As their smaller competitors go under, the large home-building companies grab more and more of the market.

Fill in the gap with an appropriate preposition: pick up pick at pick on pick out


If kids are picking…you at school, you need to tell me or your teacher about it.
If kids are picking on you at school, you need to tell me or your teacher about it.

Fill in the gap with an appropriate preposition:

go up go under go over go on 

In the competition, the judge goes…each dog and assesses it.
In the competition, the judge goes over each dog and assesses it.

Fill in the gap with an appropriate preposition:

pick up pick at pick on pick out

His story was picked… as the best by the judges.
His story was picked out as the best by the judges.

Fill in the gap with an appropriate preposition:

pick up pick at pick on pick out

Paige could only pick…. her meal, forcing down a mouthful or two.
Paige could only pick at her meal, forcing down a mouthful or two.

Put the conversation in order:
Putting you through.
Thank you.
Right, a patrol car is on its way.
Try not to touch anything, sir.
Your name and address please, sir.
Police, please.
I'd like to report a break-in at my house.
Alan Jones, 11, Maurice Crescent, Lawton.
Police, can I help you?
Emergency. Which service do you require?

1. Emergency. Which service do you require?
2. Police, can I help you?
3. Putting you through.
4. Police, please.
5. I'd like to report a break-in at my house.
6. Your name and address please, sir.
7. Right, a patrol car is on its way.
8. Try not to touch anything, sir.
9. Thank you.

Put the conversation in order:
An elderly man has fallen and he seems to have broken his arm.
Dixon's electrical store at Thistle Shopping Centre, in Stirling.
Ambulance service, what is your location?
What is the incident, madam?
Ambulance.
Emergency, which service please?
Connecting you.
Right, don't move the gentleman, don't give him anything to eat or drink, and keep him warm. An ambulance is on its way.
Thank you.

1. Emergency, which service please?
2. Ambulance service, what is your location?
3. Dixon's electrical store at Thistle Shopping Centre, in Stirling.
4. What is the incident, madam?
5. An elderly man has fallen and he seems to have broken his arm.
6. Connecting you.
7. Right, don't move the gentleman, don't give him anything to eat or drink, and keep him warm. An ambulance is on its way.
8. Thank you.
